17.reason: [Error: This condition incorrectly checks for newline character, but the string does not contain '\n' from gets(). Should check if there are more digits after the decimal point, i.e., if (n + 3 < l) to determine if a decimal point is needed.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: This condition incorrectly checks for newline instead of checking if there are more digits after the decimal point. It should check if there are remaining characters after the current position, e.g., if (n + 3 < l)
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  out-of-bounds access for small numbers (e.g., "0.5"); also uses '\n' which is not present (string from gets does not contain newline); should check if there are remaining digits.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: gets() strips the newline character, so s will not contain '\n'. Additionally, n+4 may access out-of-bounds memory for short inputs. Should check if remaining digits exist, e.g., if (n + 3 < l)]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  gets() discards '\n', so the string ends with '\0'; checking against '\n' is incorrect and accessing s[n+4] may go out of bounds. It should check if there are remaining digits (e.g., n + 3 < l)
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect boundary check: strings from gets() are null-terminated, not newline-terminated; also n+4 may be out of bounds]

6.reason: gets() is removed in the C11 standard and causes compilation errors in modern compilers; additionally, it strips the newline character, invalidating the later check for '\n'

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers. Use 'fgets' instead.]
